examples: A team of astronomers working at the European Southern Observatory in Chile, led by Simon Clark, spotted a flyer in the cosmos.
A few astronomers thought they could see even more of a dinosaur-cosmos link, a pattern of mass extinctions occurring like clockwork in the geologic record.
But such transits have paved the way for what might prove to be one of the most vital breakthroughs in the cosmos—detecting Earth-sized planets orbiting other stars.
At the same time, in abrupt digressions, the cosmos churned, oceans teemed, nebulae exploded.
Science is unraveling mysteries in the tiniest of human genes and the vast cosmos.
